title: acalypha subtomentosa
intro: Acalypha subtomentosa, also known as the soft-leaved copperleaf or soft-leaved acalypha, is a perennial plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a traditional medicinal plant that has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and soothing skin irritations. Acalypha subtomentosa can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial properties.
